observation.images.base_0_rgb.comment: Primary/exterior camera slot. float32 [0,1] CHW; the agent maps the env's exterior_image here and resizes to 224.

observation.images.left_wrist_0_rgb.comment: Wrist camera slot; the env's wrist_image maps here.

observation.images.right_wrist_0_rgb.comment: Second wrist slot (DROID has two wrist cams); the single-arm RoboLab env has no match, so the agent zero-fills it.

observation.text.comment: Task instruction (language conditioning); required by the VLA. FAST/autoregressive decoding makes pi0-FAST notably more language-steerable than the flow-matching pi0-DROID.

observation.state.comment: DROID joint-space proprio: active dims 0-6 = 7 Panda arm joint positions (rad), dim 7 = gripper opening; dims 8-31 are zero padding (paligemma backbone max_state_dim=32). QUANTILE-normalized. Joint proprio matches the RoboLab DROID env's joint state (the agent zero-pads the env's 8-dim state to 32).

action.arm_joint_vel.comment: 7 Panda arm joint VELOCITY targets (the openpi DROID action space, same as pi05_droid). The decoded action chunk is 8-DoF (7 joint vel + gripper); pairs with the env's joint_vel branch (envs/droid_joint_vel.json, JointVelocityActionCfg). OPEN: newer openpi DROID training recommends joint-POSITION actions for sim compatibility -- if a joint-pos pi0-FAST-DROID is used instead, switch to JOINT_ABS_POS and pair envs/droid_joint_pos.json.

action.gripper.comment: Absolute gripper command in [0,1] (action dim 7); the agent clips to NORM01 and the env's binary gripper closes when >0.5.

comment: pi0-FAST DROID: the autoregressive, FAST-tokenized variant of pi0 finetuned on DROID, paired with the RoboLab (Isaac Lab) DROID env (droid_franka_robotiq), ~15 Hz, pure eval / no finetuning. Verified checkpoint: openpi gs://openpi-assets/checkpoints/pi0_fast_droid (config pi0_fast_droid; an s3://openpi-assets/... mirror also exists). This is the RoboArena DROID FAST baseline and the canonical *inference* FAST checkpoint. ARCHITECTURE vs pi05_droid (flow matching): pi0-FAST is paligemma + an autoregressive action head over FAST tokens (Frequency-space Action Sequence Tokenization = per-chunk DCT + BPE over QUANTILE-normalized actions, vocab ~1024). Actions are decoded one FAST token at a time then de-tokenized back to a continuous chunk, so inference is slower than pi0/pi0.5 flow matching but follows language better. I/O mirrors pi05_droid: 3 camera slots (base + 2 wrist; the single-arm RoboLab env fills base<-exterior, left_wrist<-wrist, right_wrist zero-padded) + 32-dim padded JOINT proprio (active 8: 7 joint pos + gripper); output is an 8-DoF action chunk = 7 arm joint VELOCITIES + absolute gripper. OPEN (policy_class/loader): the JAX/openpi original is preferred over the LeRobot port for DROID (per the research report) -- to load it natively use openpi (policy.config=pi0_fast_droid). The LeRobot equivalent class is PI0FASTPolicy (policy.type=pi0_fast, action tokenizer lerobot/fast-action-tokenizer), but no verified LeRobot DROID port exists (LeRobot ships only lerobot/pi0fast-base and lerobot/pi0fast-libero, the latter known to underperform the JAX original); if a LeRobot DROID port surfaces, set checkpoint to its repo id. OPEN (chunk_size): set to 10 (LeRobot pi0-FAST default action_horizon; FAST paper DROID @15 Hz); openpi pi0_fast_droid may predict up to ~15-16 and execute a shorter open-loop horizon -- verify the loaded config's action_horizon at wire-up.
